food_subgroup: Fast food sharing meal — fried chicken with sides and beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a branded multi-component fast food sharing meal. FOODON_00002473 (fried chicken food product) is used as the closest parent anchor for the primary protein component (Chickenjoy fried chicken).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a code for a branded multi-component fast food meal set. A0CKM (Chicken meat, cooked) is used as the closest parent anchor representing the dominant protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for a branded fast food sharing meal. 255620007 (Food substance) is the broadest defensible anchor.

main_ingredients: side dishes (rice or mashed potato or coleslaw or corn)

serving.description: Full meal set for two persons: 4 pieces Chickenjoy fried chicken, 4 side dishes, 2 drinks
serving.natural_language_portion: One full sharing meal set for two people, comprising 4 pieces of Chickenjoy bone-in fried chicken (approximately 600 g), 4 side portions (approximately 400 g total, e.g. rice, mashed potato, coleslaw, or corn), and 2 drinks (approximately 200 g total), yielding an estimated total weight of approximately 1200 g for the complete set.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fried Chicken (breaded, bone-in, fast food) and component estimation for fast food meal set
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for the Jollibee Value for Two Meal A (D) as a composite product. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by weighted component analysis: (1) Chickenjoy-style breaded fried chicken (bone-in, skin-on) referenced against USDA FDC entry for fast food fried chicken (FDC ID 171477, 'Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter'); (2) typical fast food sides (steamed rice, mashed potato, coleslaw, corn) referenced against USDA SR Legacy entries; (3) carbonated soft drink referenced against USDA FDC entry for cola-type beverage. Weighted average per 100 g of the full 1200 g meal set is computed. Values represent the blended composite per 100 g of the complete meal as served. Confidence is medium due to estimation from components.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried chicken, white rice, mashed potato, coleslaw, and cola beverage
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee Value for Two Meal A (D) composite meal. GI estimated as a weighted average of the meal components using approved GI source data: breaded fried chicken GI ~60 (University of Sydney GI Database, analogous entry for battered/breaded chicken); white steamed rice GI ~72 (University of Sydney GI Database, Jasmine rice); mashed potato GI ~85 (University of Sydney GI Database); coleslaw GI ~45 (estimated low-GI vegetable salad); corn GI ~52 (University of Sydney GI Database); cola-type soft drink GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by estimated gram contribution per component in the 1200 g meal set yields a blended GI of approximately 62 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 270) / 100 = 167.4, rounded to 167.0. Carbohydrate per serving estimated at 270 g for the full 1200 g meal set (22.5 g per 100 g × 12).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 310 mg per 100 g, yielding an estimated 3720 mg sodium for the full 1200 g meal set, substantially exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal set, as the total sodium load from the fried chicken seasoning, sides, and beverages is likely to exceed the daily sodium target in a single sitting; if consumed, no additional sodium-containing foods should be eaten that day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (estimated GL ~167) driven by white rice sides, mashed potato, and sugar-sweetened beverages, combined with high total carbohydrate and added sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal set due to its very high glycemic load; if consumed on an exceptional basis, portion should be restricted to a small fraction of the set, carbohydrate-dense sides (rice, mashed potato) should be minimised, and sugar-sweetened drinks should be replaced with water or unsweetened beverages, with close post-meal blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high saturated fat from deep-fried breaded chicken skin, high sodium, high total energy, and trans fat from frying oil poses multiple cardiovascular risk factors
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal set; the deep-fried chicken contributes saturated and trans fats that raise LDL cholesterol, while the high sodium content elevates blood pressure, and the high caloric density promotes weight gain — all established cardiovascular risk factors.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jollibee Value for Two Meal A (D) is a branded fast food sharing meal set sold at Jollibee restaurants in Singapore, including the Bugis Village outlet, and listed on field survey data. The set comprises 4 pieces of Chickenjoy — Jollibee's signature bone-in, skin-on deep-fried chicken coated in a proprietary seasoned wheat flour batter — along with 4 side dish portions (which may include steamed white rice, mashed potato, coleslaw, or corn depending on the variant selected) and 2 drinks (typically carbonated soft drinks or juice). Chickenjoy is characterised by its crispy, golden-brown battered exterior and juicy, well-seasoned interior, and is a flagship product of the Filipino fast food chain Jollibee, which has a strong presence in Southeast Asia and among Filipino diaspora communities globally. The meal set is designed for sharing between two people and represents a value-oriented family-style dining option. Nutritionally, the meal is energy-dense, high in sodium, saturated fat, and refined carbohydrates, and carries a medium glycemic index (estimated ~62) with a very high glycemic load per full serving (~167) due to the combination of fried chicken, starchy sides, and sugar-sweetened beverages. The meal is certified Halal and is suitable for Muslim consumers. Clinically, this meal set is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and glycemic load. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or soy allergies. The meal is ultra-processed and should be consumed only occasionally as part of a balanced diet.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA) for the Jollibee Value for Two Meal A (D) as a composite branded meal set. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FDC entries for breaded fried chicken, white rice, mashed potato, coleslaw, corn, and cola beverage. GI is estimated as a weighted average of component GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium. A high confidence rating would require an exact match in an approved primary source or official Jollibee Singapore nutritional disclosure verified against an approved database.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the full composite meal set (estimated total weight 1200 g: ~600 g fried chicken, ~400 g sides, ~200 g beverages). 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC component entries used for estimation. 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC component entries used for estimation. 4. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are estimated from USDA FDC fried chicken entries and may vary depending on the frying oil used by Jollibee Singapore. 5. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate weighted across meal components; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite meal. 6. Glycemic load per serving of 167.0 is calculated for the full 1200 g meal set: (62 × 270 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 167.4, rounded to 167.0. 7. The meal is listed as Halal on field survey data (Jollibee Bugis Village). 8. Side dish composition may vary; the four sides in Variant D are assumed to include a combination of rice, mashed potato, coleslaw, and corn based on standard Jollibee Singapore menu descriptions. 9. Drink composition assumed to be standard cola-type carbonated soft drink; substitution with juice would alter sugar and GI values. 10. No zero-value core macros are present; all four core macros are non-zero and nutritionally justified for this composite fried chicken meal set.
